gaps: No test covers the scenario where a DFU update button remains tappable/visible after a successful Charge Case firmware update, leading to a failure on re-tap until app restart.

No test covers post-DFU button state management — verifying that the Software Update button or 'Update All' button becomes disabled, hidden, or transitions to an 'Up to Date' state immediately after a successful firmware update completes.

No test covers the user experience of tapping a stale DFU button after a completed update and verifying the app handles it gracefully (e.g., shows 'already up to date' message) rather than failing silently or requiring an app restart.

No test covers the specific device model Voyager Legend 50 (this appears to be a typo or non-existent model — the domain knowledge references Voyager Free 60+ and Voyager Legend series separately; the bug may be misattributed to a wrong device, but no test covers this exact post-update button persistence failure on any Poly TWS device with a charge case).
No test covers DFU button lifecycle across app restart — verifying that after a successful update, killing and relaunching the app correctly shows the updated state without the stale update button.

generated_testcases.title: TC-DFU-001: Verify Software Update button becomes disabled/hidden immediately after successful Charge Case firmware update
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app on the paired phone.
Tap the connected device (e.g., Voyager Free 60+ UC) to enter Device screen.
Navigate to Settings → Software Update.
Verify the 'Update All' or 'Update' button is visible and tappable (indicating an update is available).
Tap 'Update All' to begin the Charge Case firmware update.
Wait for the update to complete (observe progress reaches 100% and device restarts).
After the device reconnects, observe the Software Update screen WITHOUT leaving the app or restarting it.
Check the state of the 'Update All' / 'Update' button.
generated_testcases.expected: Immediately after the successful Charge Case firmware update completes and the device reconnects, the 'Update All' or 'Update' button should be disabled, hidden, or replaced with an 'Up to Date' / 'No Updates Available' message. The button should NOT remain tappable in the same active state as before the update.

generated_testcases.title: TC-DFU-002: Verify tapping stale DFU button after successful update shows graceful message instead of silent failure
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app and connect to the device (e.g., Voyager Free 60+ UC).
Navigate to Settings → Software Update.
Tap 'Update All' to perform a Charge Case firmware update.
Wait for the update to complete successfully (100% progress, device restart, reconnection).
Without leaving the Software Update screen, tap the 'Update All' / 'Update' button again.
Observe the app's response.
generated_testcases.expected: The app should display a graceful message such as 'Your device is already up to date' or 'No updates available'. The app should NOT show a silent failure (e.g., spinner stuck indefinitely, no feedback, or an error toast without explanation). The app should NOT require a force-kill and restart to recover from this state.

generated_testcases.title: TC-DFU-003: Verify DFU button state persists correctly across app restart after successful Charge Case update
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app and connect to the device (e.g., Voyager Free 60+ UC).
Navigate to Settings → Software Update.
Verify an update is available and tap 'Update All'.
Wait for the Charge Case firmware update to complete successfully (100% progress, device restart, reconnection).
Force-kill the Poly Studio Mobile app (swipe it away from the recent apps list).
Reopen Poly Studio Mobile app and wait for the device to reconnect.
Navigate to Settings → Software Update.
Observe the state of the 'Update All' / 'Update' button.
generated_testcases.expected: After the app restart, the Software Update screen should correctly reflect the updated state. The 'Update All' / 'Update' button should be disabled, hidden, or replaced with an 'Up to Date' message. The stale update button should NOT reappear after the app restart.

generated_testcases.title: TC-DFU-004: Verify DFU button state after successful update when navigating away and back to Software Update screen
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app and connect to the device (e.g., Voyager Free 60+ UC).
Navigate to Settings → Software Update.
Tap 'Update All' to perform a Charge Case firmware update.
Wait for the update to complete successfully (100% progress, device restart, reconnection).
Tap the back button to return to the Device screen.
Navigate back to Settings → Software Update.
Observe the state of the 'Update All' / 'Update' button.
generated_testcases.expected: After navigating away from and back to the Software Update screen, the button should reflect the updated state (disabled/hidden/'Up to Date'). The stale active update button should NOT reappear due to screen re-entry.

generated_testcases.title: TC-DFU-005: Verify DFU button state after successful update when switching between sub-settings and returning
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app and connect to the device (e.g., Voyager Free 60+ UC).
Navigate to Settings → Software Update.
Tap 'Update All' to perform a Charge Case firmware update.
Wait for the update to complete successfully (100% progress, device restart, reconnection).
Without leaving the Software Update screen, navigate to a different settings sub-screen (e.g., Audio Settings → Transparency Mode → Environment).
Navigate back to Settings → Software Update.
Observe the state of the 'Update All' / 'Update' button.
generated_testcases.expected: After navigating to another settings sub-screen and returning to Software Update, the button should correctly show the updated state (disabled/hidden/'Up to Date'). The stale active button should not reappear.

generated_testcases.title: TC-DFU-006: Verify DFU button state after successful update when Bluetooth disconnects and reconnects
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app and connect to the device (e.g., Voyager Free 60+ UC).
Navigate to Settings → Software Update.
Tap 'Update All' to perform a Charge Case firmware update.
Wait for the update to complete successfully (100% progress, device restart, reconnection).
Disconnect the device by turning off Bluetooth on the phone or putting the device in the case and closing it.
Wait 10 seconds, then reconnect the device (turn Bluetooth back on or open the case).
Wait for the app to reconnect to the device.
Navigate to Settings → Software Update.
Observe the state of the 'Update All' / 'Update' button.
generated_testcases.expected: After a Bluetooth disconnect and reconnect cycle following a successful update, the Software Update screen should correctly show the updated state. The 'Update All' / 'Update' button should be disabled, hidden, or show 'Up to Date'. The stale active button should not reappear.

generated_testcases.title: TC-DFU-007: Verify DFU button state after successful update when device powers off and on
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app and connect to the device (e.g., Voyager Free 60+ UC).
Navigate to Settings → Software Update.
Tap 'Update All' to perform a Charge Case firmware update.
Wait for the update to complete successfully (100% progress, device restart, reconnection).
Power off the device (e.g., place earbuds in case and close lid, or press and hold the case button to power off).
Wait 15 seconds, then power on the device (open case lid or press button).
Wait for the app to reconnect to the device.
Navigate to Settings → Software Update.
Observe the state of the 'Update All' / 'Update' button.
generated_testcases.expected: After a device power-off and power-on cycle following a successful update, the Software Update screen should correctly show the updated state. The 'Update All' / 'Update' button should be disabled, hidden, or show 'Up to Date'.

generated_testcases.title: TC-DFU-008: Verify DFU button state after successful update when phone is rotated (configuration change)
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app and connect to the device (e.g., Voyager Free 60+ UC).
Navigate to Settings → Software Update.
Tap 'Update All' to perform a Charge Case firmware update.
Wait for the update to complete successfully (100% progress, device restart, reconnection).
Rotate the phone from portrait to landscape (or vice versa) to trigger a configuration change / activity recreation.
Observe the Software Update screen after rotation.
Check the state of the 'Update All' / 'Update' button.
generated_testcases.expected: After a phone rotation (configuration change) following a successful update, the Software Update screen should correctly show the updated state. The 'Update All' / 'Update' button should be disabled, hidden, or show 'Up to Date'. The stale active button should not reappear due to activity recreation.

generated_testcases.title: TC-DFU-009: Verify DFU button state after successful update when app is backgrounded and foregrounded
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app and connect to the device (e.g., Voyager Free 60+ UC).
Navigate to Settings → Software Update.
Tap 'Update All' to perform a Charge Case firmware update.
Wait for the update to complete successfully (100% progress, device restart, reconnection).
Press the Home button to background the app.
Wait 30 seconds, then reopen the app from the app launcher or recent apps.
Wait for the app to reconnect to the device.
Observe the Software Update screen.
Check the state of the 'Update All' / 'Update' button.
generated_testcases.expected: After backgrounding and foregrounding the app following a successful update, the Software Update screen should correctly show the updated state. The 'Update All' / 'Update' button should be disabled, hidden, or show 'Up to Date'.

generated_testcases.title: TC-DFU-010: Verify DFU button state after successful update when tapping button rapidly multiple times
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app and connect to the device (e.g., Voyager Free 60+ UC).
Navigate to Settings → Software Update.
Tap 'Update All' to perform a Charge Case firmware update.
Wait for the update to complete successfully (100% progress, device restart, reconnection).
Immediately after reconnection, rapidly tap the 'Update All' / 'Update' button 5 times in quick succession (within 2 seconds).
Observe the app's response after each tap and after the rapid tapping stops.
generated_testcases.expected: The app should handle rapid tapping gracefully. Either the button should be disabled after the first tap (preventing further taps), or each tap should show a consistent 'already up to date' message. The app should NOT crash, freeze, or enter an unrecoverable state. No silent failures should occur.
